title: Hot Honey
tagline: Sweet heat that goes on everything. The condiment that defined 2025.
description: Honey infused with chili peppers for the perfect balance of sweet and spicy. Drizzle on pizza, fried chicken, biscuits, or cheese.
ingredients: 2-4 Tbsp hot sauce (like Frank's or Cholula)
1/4 tsp cayenne pepper (optional, for extra heat)
steps: Combine honey, hot sauce, red pepper flakes, and cayenne (if using) in a small saucepan.
Heat over low heat for 5-7 minutes, stirring occasionally. Don't let it boil.
Remove from heat and stir in apple cider vinegar.
Let cool slightly, then taste and adjust heat level.
Transfer to a jar. Store at room temperature up to 1 month.
notes: Heat levels: Start with 2 Tbsp hot sauce and add more to taste. You can always add heat, can't take it away.
Fresno or serrano: For a fresher kick, slice 1-2 fresh chilis and simmer them in the honey instead of hot sauce.
Best on: Pizza (especially pepperoni), fried chicken, biscuits, bacon, cheese boards, vanilla ice cream.
Vinegar: Adds depth and cuts the sweetness. Don't skip it.
No-cook version: Just whisk ingredients together without heating. Flavor won't meld as well but works in a pinch.
